content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of length NNN.

Your task is to find any three indices i,j,ki, j, ki,j,k such that:


1‚Č§i,j,k‚Č§N1 \le i, j, k \le N1‚Č§i,j,k‚Č§N
i‚Č†j,i‚Č†k,j‚Č†ki \ne j, i \ne k, j \ne kiÓÄ†=j,iÓÄ†=k,jÓÄ†=k

That is, the indices {i,j,k}\{i, j, k\}{i,j,k} are pairwise distinct.
Ai+Aj‚Č†AkA_i + A_j \ne A_kAi‚Äč+Aj‚ÄčÓÄ†=Ak‚Äč
If there are multiple possible solutions, you may find any of them.

If no solution exists, print ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the length of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case,



If no solution exists, output the single integer ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.
Otherwise, output three distinct space-separated integers i,j,ki, j, ki,j,k denoting the indices you found.

If there are multiple solutions, any of them will be accepted.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: With (i,j,k)=(4,2,1)(i, j, k) = (4, 2, 1)(i,j,k)=(4,2,1), we have A4=0,A2=1,A1=2A_4 = 0, A_2 = 1, A_1 = 2A4‚Äč=0,A2‚Äč=1,A1‚Äč=2.
Because 0+1‚Č†20+1\ne 20+1ÓÄ†=2, this is a valid triplet.
There are many several other valid solutions, which will all be accepted as well.
Test case 222: It can be verified that no valid triplet of indices exists.
Test case 333: Consider (i,j,k)=(2,3,1)(i, j, k) = (2, 3, 1)(i,j,k)=(2,3,1), which gives Ai+Aj=A2+A3=2A_i + A_j = A_2 + A_3 = 2Ai‚Äč+Aj‚Äč=A2‚Äč+A3‚Äč=2 while Ak=A1=4A_k = A_1 = 4Ak‚Äč=A1‚Äč=4.
Because 2‚Č†42\ne 42ÓÄ†=4 this is a valid triplet.
